data: Learn how to handle interruptions politely

message:
Hey! Today we're learning how to handle interruptions politely in conversations.

Responding calmly to interruptions shows confidence and professionalism.
image_url: https://r2-activity-data.gosupernova.live/system-uploads/scenario-data/e38a92a9-01d0-408b-8043-b6f538f2c9e3-56.-Handling-Interruptions.jpg
button_text: Cool!

message:
When someone interrupts you, use this structure to respond:

"I understand [point], and if I may just add [response]."

For example: "I understand your concern, and if I may just add that we have a solution ready."
image_url: https://r2-activity-data.gosupernova.live/system-uploads/scenario-data/e38a92a9-01d0-408b-8043-b6f538f2c9e3-56.-Handling-Interruptions.jpg
button_text: Got it!

message:
Here's another example.

If someone cuts in during a budget discussion, you can say:

"I understand your point about the budget, and if I may just add that we have a lower-cost option."
image_url: https://r2-activity-data.gosupernova.live/system-uploads/scenario-data/e38a92a9-01d0-408b-8043-b6f538f2c9e3-56.-Handling-Interruptions.jpg
button_text: Next!

message:
This structure acknowledges what the other person said before adding your point.

It keeps the conversation respectful and shows that you are listening.
image_url: https://r2-activity-data.gosupernova.live/system-uploads/scenario-data/e38a92a9-01d0-408b-8043-b6f538f2c9e3-56.-Handling-Interruptions.jpg
button_text: Makes sense!

message:
Let's practice handling interruptions!

image_url:
button_text: Start Practice!

Select the correct way to handle an interruption politely

1.I understand your concern, and if I may just add that we have a solution ready.
2.Please stop interrupting me because I have not finished what I was saying.

Correct answer: 1

Select the correct way to respond after being interrupted

1.I understand the deadline is tight, and if I may just add that we can split the work.
2.I know the deadline is tight but you did not have to cut me off like that.

Correct answer: 1

I&&understand the&&deadline is&&tight, and&&if&&I&&may&&just&&add that&&we&&can&&split the&&work.

I&&understand the&&issue&&you&&raised, and&&if&&I&&may&&just&&add that&&we&&are&&already aware&&of it.

first_message: That was incredible! Now we're going to practice by answering few questions in English. Can we start?
prompt_id: 4d3f5213-fcc4-40f3-879d-051908377a31
questions: "'1. A colleague interrupts you during a presentation. How do you handle the interruption politely?'

'2. Someone cuts in while you are explaining your idea in a meeting. How do you respond?'

'3. A team member interrupts your update during a discussion. How do you handle it?'

'4. Your manager interrupts you while you are sharing a report. How do you respond politely?'

'5. A colleague keeps speaking over you in a group conversation. How do you handle it?'"
